build:
Alias: b
Usage: build [OPTIONS]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Compile the current package
check:
Alias: c
Usage: check [OPTIONS]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Analyze the current package and report errors, but don't build object files
doc:
Alias: d
Usage: doc [OPTIONS]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Build this package's and its dependencies' documentation
new:
Usage: new [OPTIONS] <path>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Create a new cargo package
init:
Usage: init [OPTIONS] [path]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Create a new cargo package in an existing directory
add:
Usage: add [OPTIONS] ...
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Add dependencies to a manifest file
remove:
Usage: remove [OPTIONS] <DEP_ID>...
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Remove dependencies from a manifest file
run:
Alias: r
Usage: run [OPTIONS] [args]...
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Run a binary or example of the local package
test:
Alias: t
Usage: test [OPTIONS] [TESTNAME] [-- [args]...]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Run the tests
update:
Usage: update [OPTIONS]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Update dependencies listed in Cargo.lock
publish:
Usage: publish [OPTIONS]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Package and upload this package to the registry
install:
Usage: install [OPTIONS] [crate]...
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Install a Rust binary. Default location is $HOME/.cargo/bin
clippy:
Usage: cargo clippy [options] [--] [<opts>...]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Checks a package to catch common mistakes and improve your Rust code.
clippy --no-deps: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Run Clippy only on the given crate, without linting the dependencies
clippy --fix: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Automatically apply lint suggestions. This flag implies `--no-deps` and `--all-targets`
clippy --explain:
Usage: --explain LINT
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Print the documentation for a given lint
fetch:
Usage: fetch [OPTIONS]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Fetch dependencies of a package from the network
fetch --target:
Usage: --target <TRIPLE>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Fetch dependencies for the target triple
fix:
Usage: cargo.exe fix [OPTIONS]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Automatically fix lint warnings reported by rustc
fix --edition: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Fix in preparation for the next edition
fix --edition-idioms: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Fix warnings to migrate to the idioms of an edition
fix --broken-code: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Fix code even if it already has compiler errors
fix --allow-no-vcs: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Fix code even if a VCS was not detected
fix --allow-dirty: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Fix code even if the working directory is dirty
fix --allow-staged: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Fix code even if the working directory has staged changes
fix --test:
Usage: --test [<NAME>]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Fix only the specified test target
fix --bench:
Usage: --bench [<NAME>]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Fix only the specified bench target
fix --release:
Alias: -r
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Fix artifacts in release mode, with optimizations
fmt:
Usage: Usage: cargo fmt [OPTIONS] [-- <rustfmt_options>...]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Formats all bin and lib files of the current crate using rustfmt.
fmt --all: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Format all packages, and also their local path-based dependencies
generate-lockfile:
Usage: generate-lockfile [OPTIONS]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Generate the lockfile for a package
help:
Usage: help <COMMAND>
Displays help for a cargo subcommand
locate-project:
locate-project [OPTIONS]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Print a JSON representation of a Cargo.toml file's location
login:
Usage: login [OPTIONS] [token] [-- [args]...]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Log in to a registry
logout:
Usage: logout [OPTIONS]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Remove an API token from the registry locally
metadata:
Usage: metadata [OPTIONS]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Output the resolved dependencies of a package, the concrete used versions including overrides, in machine-readable format
metadata --filter-platform:
Usage: --filter-platform <TRIPLE>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Only include resolve dependencies matching the given target-triple
metadata --no-deps: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Output information only about the workspace members and don't fetch dependencies
owner:
Usage: owner [OPTIONS] [crate]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Manage the owners of a crate on the registry
owner --add:
Alias: -a
Usage: --add <LOGIN>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Name of a user or team to invite as an owner
owner --remove:
Alias: -r
Usage: --remove <LOGIN>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Name of a user or team to remove as an owner
owner --index:
Usage: --index <INDEX>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Registry index to modify owners for
owner --token:
Usage: --token <TOKEN>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}API token to use when authenticating
package:
Usage: package [OPTIONS]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Assemble the local package into a distributable tarball
package --list:
Alias: -l
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Print files included in a package without making one
package --no-verify: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Don't verify the contents by building them
package --no-metadata: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Ignore warnings about a lack of human-usable metadata
package --allow-dirty: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Allow dirty working directories to be packaged
package --workspace: Assemble all packages in the workspace
pkgid:
Usage: pkgid [OPTIONS] [spec]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Print a fully qualified package specification
pkgid --package:
Alias: -p
Usage: --package [<SPEC>]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Argument to get the package ID specifier for
read-manifest:
Usage: read-manifest [OPTIONS]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Print a JSON representation of a Cargo.toml manifest.
report:
Usage: report [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Generate and display various kinds of reports
report future-incompatibilities: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Reports any crates which will eventually stop compiling
rustc:
Usage: rustc [OPTIONS] [args]...
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Compile a package, and pass extra options to the compiler
rustc --crate-type:
Usage: --crate-type <CRATE-TYPE>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Comma separated list of types of crates for the compiler to emit
rustdoc:
Usage: rustdoc [OPTIONS] [args]...
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Build a package's documentation, using specified custom flags.
rustdoc --open: Opens the docs in a browser after the operation
tree:
Usage: tree [OPTIONS]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Display a tree visualization of a dependency graph
tree --edges:
Alias: -e
Usage: --edges <KINDS>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}The kinds of dependencies to display (features,normal,build,dev,all,no-normal,no-build,no-dev,no-proc-macro)
tree --invert:
Alias: -i
Usage: --invert [<SPEC>]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Invert the tree direction and focus on the given package
tree --prune:
Usage: --prune <SPEC>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Prune the given package from the display of the dependency tree
tree --depth:
Usage: --depth <DEPTH>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Maximum display depth of the dependency tree
tree --prefix:
Usage: --prefix <PREFIX>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Change the prefix (indentation) of how each entry is displayed [default: indent] [possible values: depth, indent, none]
tree --duplicates:
Alias: -d
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Show only dependencies which come in multiple versions (implies -i)
tree --charset:
Usage: --charset <CHARSET>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Character set to use in output [default: utf8] [possible values: utf8, ascii]
tree --format:
Alias: -f
Usage: --format <FORMAT>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Format string used for printing dependencies [default: {p}]
tree --package:
Alias: -p
Usage: --package [<SPEC>]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Package to be used as the root of the tree
tree --workspace: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Display the tree for all packages in the workspace
tree --target:
Usage: --target <TRIPLE>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Filter dependencies matching the given target-triple (default host platform). Pass `all` to include all targets
vendor:
Usage: cargo.exe vendor [OPTIONS] [path]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Vendor all dependencies for a project locally
vendor --no-delete: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Don't delete older crates in the vendor directory
vendor --sync:
Alias: -s
Usage: --sync <TOML>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Additional `Cargo.toml` to sync and vendor
verify-project:
Usage: verify-project [OPTIONS]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Check correctness of crate manifest
yank:
Usage: yank [OPTIONS] [crate]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Remove a pushed crate from the index
yank --version:
Usage: --version <VERSION>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}The version to yank or un-yank
yank --undo: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Undo a yank, putting a version back into the index
yank --token:
Usage: --token <TOKEN>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}API token to use when authenticating
build --package:
Alias: -p
Usage: --package [<SPEC>]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Package to build (see 'cargo help pkgid')
check --test:
Usage: --test [<NAME>]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Check only the specified test target
check --bench:
Usage: --bench [<NAME>]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Check only the specified bench target
clean -doc: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Whether or not to clean just the documentation directory
clean --package:
Alias: -p
Usage: --package [<SPEC>]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Package to clean artifacts for
clean --release:
Alias: -r
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Whether or not to clean release artifacts
clean --profile:
Usage: --profile <PROFILE-NAME>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Clean artifacts of the specified profile
clean --target:
Usage: --target <TRIPLE>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Target triple to clean output for
doc --open: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Opens the docs in a browser after the operation
add --path:
Usage: --path <PATH>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Filesystem path to local crate to add
add --branch:
Usage: --branch <BRANCH>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Git branch to download the crate from
add --tag:
Usage: --tag <TAG>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Git tag to download the crate from
add --rev:
Usage: --rev <REV>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Git reference to download the crate from
add --target:
Usage: --target <TARGET>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Add as dependency to the given target platform
remove --package:
Usage: --target <TARGET>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Remove as dependency from the given target platform
run --package:
Alias: -p
Usage: --package [<SPEC>]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Package with the target to run
run --bin:
Usage: --bin <NAME>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Name of the bin target to run
run --example:
Usage: --example [<NAME>]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Name of the example target to run
test --doc: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Whether or not to clean just the documentation directory
test --package:
Alias: -p
Usage: --package [<SPEC>]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Package to run tests for
test --lib: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Test only this package's library unit tests
test --test:
Usage: --test [<NAME>]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Test only the specified test target
test --bench:
Usage: --bench [<NAME>]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Test only the specified bench target
bench --test:
Usage: --test [<NAME>]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Benchmark only the specified test target
bench --bench:
Usage: --bench [<NAME>]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Benchmark only the specified bench target
update --aggressive: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Force updating all dependencies of SPEC as well when used with -p
update --precise:
Usage: --precise <PRECISE>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Update a single dependency to exactly PRECISE when used with -p
search --limit:
Usage: --limit <LIMIT>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Limit the number of results (default: 10, max: 100)
search --index:
Usage: --index <INDEX>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Registry index URL to upload the package to
publish --index:
Usage: --index <INDEX>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Registry index URL to upload the package to
publish --no-verify: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Don't verify the contents by building them
publish --allow-dirty: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Allow dirty working directories to be packaged
install --git:
Usage: --git <URL>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Git URL to install the specified crate from
install --branch:
Usage: --branch <BRANCH>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Branch to use when installing from git
install --tag:
Usage: --tag <TAG>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Tag to use when installing from git
install --rev:
Usage: --rev <SHA>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Specific commit to use when installing from git
install --path:
Usage: --path <PATH>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Filesystem path to local crate to install
install --force:
Alias: -f
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Force overwriting existing crates or binaries
install --list: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}list all installed packages and their versions
install --profile:
Usage: --profile <PROFILE-NAME>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Install artifacts with the specified profile

cargo_core_info.__future_incompat_report: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Outputs a future incompatibility report at the end of the build
cargo_core_info.__test:
Usage: --test [<NAME>]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Build only the specified test target
cargo_core_info.__bench:
Usage: --bench [<NAME>]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Build only the specified bench target
cargo_core_info.__features:
Alias: -F
Usage: --features <FEATURES>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Space or comma separated list of features to activate
cargo_core_info.__jobs:
Alias: -j
Usage: --jobs <N>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Number of parallel jobs, defaults to # of CPUs.
cargo_core_info.__keep_going: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Do not abort the build as soon as there is an error (unstable)
cargo_core_info.__release:
Alias: -r
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Build artifacts in release mode, with optimizations
cargo_core_info.__release_check:
Alias: -r
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Check artifacts in release mode, with optimizations
cargo_core_info.__profile:
Usage: --profile <PROFILE-NAME>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Build artifacts with the specified profile
cargo_core_info.__profile_check:
Usage: --profile <PROFILE-NAME>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Check artifacts with the specified profile
cargo_core_info.__out_dir:
Usage: --out-dir <PATH>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Copy final artifacts to this directory (unstable)
cargo_core_info.__vcs:
Usage: --vcs <VCS>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Initialize a new repository for the given version control system (git, hg, pijul, or fossil) or do not initialize any version control at all (none), overriding a global configuration. [possible values: git, hg, pijul, fossil, none]
cargo_core_info.__edition:
Usage: -edition <YEAR>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Edition to set for the crate generated [possible values: 2015, 2018, 2021]
cargo_core_info.__name:
Usage: --name <NAME>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Set the resulting package name, defaults to the directory name

common_options.--version:
Alias: -V
Print version info and exit
common_options.--verbose:
Alias: -v
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Use verbose output (-vv very verbose/build.rs output)
common_options.--quiet:
Alias: -q
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Do not print cargo log messages
common_options.-C:
Usage: -C <DIRECTORY>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Change to DIRECTORY before doing anything(nightly-only)
common_options.--frozen: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Require Cargo.lock and cache are up to date
common_options.-Z:
Usage: -Z <FLAG>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Unstable (nightly-only) flags to Cargo, see 'cargo -Z help' for details
